channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: ‘The time to do something is now’: Ontario looking at changes to online gambling ads
Ontario education minister urges parents not to pull kids from school for sports
item.description: Ontario Education Minister Paul Calandra is urging parents not to pull their kids out of school for sports tournaments next year, when new attendance rules begin.

item.title: ‘Lasting prosperity in both regions:’ Ontario inks agreement with Utah to boost trade
item.description: Ontario has signed a memorandum of understanding with Utah to boost trade, marking the latest in a series of similar agreements that have been inked with individual U.S. states since the onset of the trade war.

item.title: Hundreds of Ontario parents were denied pandemic payments they were entitled to. Here is why
item.description: Hundreds of Ontario parents did not receive payments that they were entitled to during the COVID-19 pandemic because the money was sent to someone else instead, a new report from the province’s acting ombudsman states.

item.title: ‘We’re running blind’: Ontario pharmacist sounds alarm on drug shortages
item.description: A Toronto-area pharmacists says patients are showing up with prescriptions for common drugs to treat glaucoma, post-surgery pain, and heart burn, but they're backordered and in many cases the alternatives are hard to come by.

item.title: New rules for Ontario auto insurance could leave drivers exposed experts warn
item.description: Ontario drivers will have new decisions to make when renewing their auto insurance policies as the provincial government rolls out major changes.

item.title: Ontario is raising the speed limit on more highways. Here are the corridors getting a new 110 km/h limit
item.description: Drivers will soon be able to legally go faster on more stretches of Ontario’s highway network.

item.title: ‘I’m no Justin Trudeau’: Doug Ford says sagging poll numbers not indicative of his popularity
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford pushed back again Tuesday against the idea that a number of recent polls indicate his popularity is declining as he closes in on a decade in power.

item.title: Several First Nations sign deal with Ottawa, Ontario to own part of a nuclear reactor
item.description: Several Ontario First Nations are set to become owners of a new nuclear reactor after striking a commercial partnership with the federal and provincial governments.

item.title: Ontario signs critical minerals agreement with the UK
item.description: Ontario has signed an agreement with the United Kingdom aimed at opening international markets for critical minerals. The deal could create opportunities for projects like Canada Nickel’s Crawford development near Timmins.

item.title: Ontario's repealed wage restraint law Bill 124 still rippling through public sector
item.description: Ontario’s ill-fated wage restraint law known as Bill 124 is still sending ripples through the public sector across the province, even years after the law was repealed, with the electricity system now attempting to untangle the fallout

item.title: Ontario doctors voice concern on emergency department wait times
item.description: The Ontario Medical Association (OMA) is warning that the province’s emergency departments are already facing crowding as the busy summer season begins.

item.title: Ontario families struggling as community and social service workers strike enters fourth week
item.description: For the last three weeks, Toronto resident Owen Rees has filled notebooks with pleas to return home to his roomful of records, his friends down the hall, and a dance party every Friday.

item.title: NDP calls for audit into $1 billion given to private administrators of public services
item.description: Ontario’s opposition NDP is calling for the province’s Auditor General to audit three private companies who together got $1 billion to administer public services, including employment services and disability support.

item.title: Ontario adds 2 major ticket resale sites to consumer watchlist
item.description: A day after they were added to a provincially run consumer safety watchlist, Ticket resale sites SeatGeek and StubHub are saying the government has failed to provide them with enough clarity around a new law banning the resale of tickets above face value.
